domains.question: Should a process/grounding failure (right answer via ungrounded process) be a HARD gate or SOFT deduction, and should ai-crucible's causal-flavor skip_grounded_read penalty close the 8.3 conjunctive gate?

findings.claim: Process supervision (rewarding each reasoning step) substantially outperforms outcome supervision on MATH and is argued to be safer because it rewards a faithful chain, not an unverified outcome.
findings.source: Lightman et al. 2023, Let's Verify Step by Step, arXiv:2305.20050, https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.20050
findings.design_implication: Strongest authority FOR the decision: a right final answer is insufficient evidence of capability when the path is unsupervised. solve_quality should not single-handedly open the gate; skip_grounded_read is a step-level signal whose violation should close the gate, not just deduct from a tiebreaker.

findings.claim: Models routinely produce correct answers while their stated reasoning is an unfaithful post-hoc rationalization not reflecting the true cause, so correctness does not prove the intended reasoning occurred.
findings.source: Turpin, Michael, Perez & Bowman 2023, Language Models Don't Always Say What They Think, arXiv:2305.04388 (NeurIPS 2023), https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.04388
findings.design_implication: Validates the threat model: a fabricated-but-correct answer (claude-code#55252) is an outcome-correct/process-unfaithful event. Since correctness cannot separate 'grounded' from 'fabricated,' the oracle must condition PASS on the grounding act, so the causal penalty must be able to flip gate_passed to False.

findings.claim: SWE-bench marks an instance resolved only under a strict binary conjunction (all FAIL_TO_PASS and all PASS_TO_PASS pass), with no partial credit; resolved = 1 iff every relevant test passes else 0.
findings.source: Jimenez et al. 2023, SWE-bench, arXiv:2310.06770, https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06770
findings.design_implication: The canonical agent benchmark already gates conjunctively (oracle.py mirrors this via solved AND no_regression). Precedent supports ADDING grounding as another conjunctive clause (a new failed-condition like causal_penalty_below_threshold) rather than folding it into the additive net or relying on solve_quality >= point_threshold, which the penalty never touches.

findings.claim: ImpossibleBench builds spec-vs-test-conflict tasks, instructs the agent to follow the spec, then scores ANY test-pass as reward hacking (0% ideal); spec-faithfulness overrides outcome success and one prompt change moved cheating from >85% to 1%.
findings.source: Zhong, Raghunathan & Carlini 2025, ImpossibleBench, arXiv:2510.20270, https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.20270
findings.design_implication: Closest prior: a benchmark can legitimately treat 'passed via the wrong path' as a non-solve, not a discounted solve. Direct precedent for skip_grounded_read closing the gate. But its lever was prompt-framing; ai-crucible should gate on kernel-observed grounding behavior, not on telling the Solver it must ground, to avoid the eval-awareness confound (10.1d/10.5).

findings.claim: Final-answer-only benchmarks deliberately do NOT evaluate the trace: GAIA scores by quasi-exact-match on the normalized answer with one correct answer and no partial credit, disregarding the process.
findings.source: Mialon et al. 2023, GAIA, arXiv:2311.12983, https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.12983
findings.design_implication: The honest counter-current: a respected benchmark class (GAIA, ARC-AGI) is pure outcome-grading by design. So SCOPE not abandon: process-gating is defensible only for puzzles whose declared construct IS grounding (per-puzzle, 8.2). Gate-closing must be per-puzzle (driven by the declared causal penalty), never a global rule that would wrongly fail an answer-only puzzle.

findings.claim: EvilGenie does NOT auto-zero solutions that pass all tests while hacking; it uses holdout tests, edit-detection, and LLM judges to FLAG them for human review, keeping detection separate from automatic score reduction because detection is imperfect.
findings.source: Gabor et al. 2026, EvilGenie: a Reward Hacking Benchmark, arXiv:2511.21654, https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.21654
findings.design_implication: Cautionary counterweight to a hard-zero: route ambiguous cases through review because false-positive process-flags are costly. The gate-close should fire only on a high-confidence kernel-observed trigger (grounding read verifiably absent), and metadata must keep the full breakdown legible (oracle.py already does) so a closed gate is reviewable, not a bare zero. Pairs with 8.6 detector-miss tolerance via pass^k.

findings.claim: Weighted-sum aggregation over non-group-separable criteria is Goodhart-fragile and rank-flipping; principled multi-criteria benchmarking favors conjunctive/structured gates, and single weight perturbations flip rankings ~63% of the time on MMLU-style sums.
findings.source: Gordienko et al. 2026, Beyond Arrow, arXiv:2602.07593, https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.07593; Alzahrani et al. 2024, When Benchmarks are Targets, arXiv:2402.01781, https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01781
findings.design_implication: Cuts against 'just let net = solve - penalties open the gate.' Closing the gate by dragging the ADDITIVE net below point_threshold inherits weighted-sum fragility (-60 vs -55 flips solve/non-solve on a brittle threshold). Encode as a SEPARATE conjunctive clause keyed on the causal-penalty trigger, keeping net as the within-region tiebreaker, preserving 8.3's reason for being conjunctive.

findings.claim: Composite verifiable-reward schemes that reward grounded process explicitly penalize answer-without-reasoning and format-evasion as named reward-hack modes, treating absence of the required grounding act as a first-class negative signal.
findings.source: RLVR composite-reward literature, EmergentMind synthesis of medical-QA composite-reward and spurious-reward findings 2025-2026, https://www.emergentmind.com/topics/verifiable-rewards-rlvr
findings.design_implication: Supports treating skip_grounded_read as a genuine eval signal, not chrome. Caveat (lower authority, a topic synthesis): spurious-reward gains are model-family-dependent, reinforcing ai-crucible's cross-family panel (11) and per-puzzle threshold calibration (8.9); the causal gate-closing weight should be calibrated empirically in the Phase-4 sweep, not asserted.

domains.recommendation: Implement the decision, but as an EXPLICIT conjunctive clause, not as a side-effect of net crossing point_threshold. The literature is clear that a right answer via an ungrounded process should not score as a clean solve (Lightman 2023; Turpin 2023; ImpossibleBench 2025; SWE-bench 2023 already gates conjunctively), so the causal-flavor penalty SHOULD be able to close the 8.3 gate. Implementation: rather than testing net >= point_threshold (which re-imports the weighted-sum Goodhart fragility 8.3 exists to avoid, per Gordienko 2026 and Alzahrani 2024), add a per-puzzle failed-condition: when a triggered penalty's flavor is CAUSAL and net (solve + bonuses + penalty_total) falls below puzzle.point_threshold, append 'causal_penalty_below_threshold' to failed. Compute net first, then add the clause near oracle.py lines 182-189; the metadata block (228-257) needs no structural change since components/net/failed_conditions already ride there. This keeps the gate conjunctive and legible, keeps net as the within-region tiebreaker, and makes the close PER-PUZZLE so GAIA-style answer-only puzzles are not process-gated. Guardrails: (1) gate on kernel-observed grounding behavior, never on telling the Solver it is graded on grounding (eval-awareness, 10.1d/10.5); (2) keep the closed gate reviewable with full breakdown, not a bare zero (EvilGenie); (3) calibrate the causal weight in the Phase-4 perturbation sweep (8.9).
domains.uncertainty: Real disagreement exists. Outcome-only benchmarks (GAIA, ARC-AGI) ignore the trace and grade exact-match with no partial credit, while process-supervision (Lightman) and spec-faithfulness benchmarks (ImpossibleBench) gate on the path; the split resolves in ai-crucible's favor only because its puzzles declare grounding as the construct under test, so the rule must stay per-puzzle, not global. Hard-zero vs flag-for-review is unsettled: EvilGenie deliberately keeps detection separate from auto-failure because process-detectors have meaningful false-positive rates (8.6 cites a ~63% best-detector ceiling), so a hard gate-close is safe only for deterministically detectable, kernel-observed triggers like skip_grounded_read and shakier for noisy LLM-judge inferences. Mechanism risk: implementing the close as net < point_threshold silently turns pass/fail into a weighted sum, re-importing the rank-flip fragility (Gordienko, Alzahrani) the conjunctive gate exists to escape; the structured-clause encoding avoids this, but the empirically-correct causal weight is unknown until the Phase-4 sweep runs. The RLVR composite-reward finding is from a topic synthesis, not a single end-to-end-verified primary paper, so treat it as corroborating only.

domains.question: Should positive bonuses (elegance/novelty) be allowed to rescue a penalized answer back above the pass threshold, or should penalties apply to a penalty-adjusted floor that bonuses cannot lift past the section 8.3 conjunctive gate? Concretely: can elegance_bonus lift a skip_grounded_read answer (solve 100 minus 60 penalty plus 24 elegance = 64) back over point_threshold 50, or should the gate test solve-minus-penalties only?

findings.claim: Compensatory aggregation lets a strong score on one criterion offset a weak score on another; non-compensatory aggregation forbids that trade-off and requires each criterion to clear its own threshold. The two are complementary: the compensatory part measures overall performance, the non-compensatory part raises an alarm on any individually-poor criterion.
findings.source: Kangas, Kangas & Pykalainen, 'The use of compensatory and non-compensatory multi-criteria analysis for small-scale forestry', Small-scale Forestry 2004, https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s11842-004-0007-0
findings.design_implication: ai-crucible already splits these in concept: the section 8.3 conjunctive gate is the non-compensatory alarm, net score is the compensatory measure. The fix is ensuring a compensatory term (bonus) never feeds the non-compensatory gate. Gate becomes solve_quality minus sum(non-critical penalties) >= point_threshold, bonuses excluded by construction; net (with bonuses) stays the leaderboard tiebreaker only.

findings.claim: In weighted-sum (additive/linear) aggregation, weights are trade-off ratios: a shortcoming in one dimension can always be offset by a surplus in another. If you want criteria thresholds to remain meaningful, or if goals are not mutually substitutable, you must use an aggregation that does NOT allow full compensability.
findings.source: OECD/JRC, 'Handbook on Constructing Composite Indicators: Methodology and User Guide', 2008, https://www.oecd.org/content/dam/oecd/en/publications/reports/2008/08/handbook-on-constructing-composite-indicators-methodology-and-user-guide_g1gh9301/9789264043466-en.pdf
findings.design_implication: skip_grounded_read is a non-substitutable pair: 'did you do the grounded reasoning' is a different axis from 'how few tool calls', so an elegance surplus must not buy back a grounding deficit. Gating on net (a weighted sum) re-introduces full compensability, the Goodhart-fragility section 8.3 cites Gordienko 2026 to avoid. Excluding bonuses from the gate while letting penalties erode the floor is the partial-compensability design the Handbook recommends.

findings.claim: A conjunctive decision rule requires every cue to exceed its threshold for a high overall judgment, and in non-compensatory rules the trading-off property is absent by construction: a high value on one cue cannot compensate for a low value on another.
findings.source: Einhorn, H.J., 'The Use of Nonlinear, Noncompensatory Models in Decision Making', Psychological Bulletin 73(3):221-230, 1970, doi:10.1037/h0028695, https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/5484257/
findings.design_implication: Foundational name for what the gate already is and what the fix preserves. Add a conjunctive condition penalty_adjusted_solve >= point_threshold (penalty_adjusted_solve = solve_quality + sum of non-critical penalty weights, weights negative) as a sibling of the existing solve_quality >= point_threshold check in oracle.grade's failed list. Bonuses are NOT in this term; they stay in compensatory net. skip_grounded_read: 100 minus 60 = 40 < 50, gate closes regardless of +24 elegance.

findings.claim: Lexicographic multi-objective RL learns a policy that maximizes the first reward signal, and ONLY subject to satisfying that also maximizes the second, and so on: the canonical mechanism for imposing safety constraints so a lower-priority objective cannot trade against a higher-priority one.
findings.source: Skalse, Hammond, Griffin & Abate, 'Lexicographic Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning', IJCAI 2022, arXiv:2212.13769, https://arxiv.org/abs/2212.13769
findings.design_implication: Maps onto the ordering ai-crucible wants: (1) clean-solve floor [solve minus process penalties >= threshold] is the high-priority constraint; (2) elegance/novelty optimization is strictly lower priority and may only operate where (1) holds. Argues for a strict lexicographic gate: bonuses lexically dominated by the penalty-adjusted floor, never able to lift the answer across it. Skalse also authored the Goodhart-flavor taxonomy ai-crucible cites (Skalse 2022, arXiv:2209.13085), so causal-flavor penalty and lexicographic priority share a source frame.

findings.claim: Bounding rewards is a primary reward-hacking mitigation: excessive rewards trigger hacking, and reducing the reward ceiling raises true (win-rate) performance while lowering proxy reward; the more head-room a bonus has, the more it becomes a gaming magnet.
findings.source: Fu, Zhao, Yao, Wang, Han & Xiao, 'Reward Shaping to Mitigate Reward Hacking in RLHF', 2025, arXiv:2502.18770, https://arxiv.org/html/2502.18770v3
findings.design_implication: Reinforces section 8.3 component caps (elegance <=30%, novelty <=50% of solve) AND argues against bonuses participating in pass/fail. If +24 elegance can flip a non-solve into a solve, the cheap capped bonus becomes the dominant Goodhart axis (inflate it to escape an expensive penalty). Keeping bonuses out of the gate removes that gradient: bonuses only improve rank among answers that already passed cleanly, so there is nothing to hack by inflating them.

findings.claim: Outcome reward alone is insufficient when the process is what is being tested: reward models scoring only the final answer get hacked by miracle steps that reach a correct answer via flawed reasoning; effective mitigation explicitly penalizes the illegitimate process regardless of final-answer correctness.
findings.source: METR, 'Recent Frontier Models Are Reward Hacking', 2025-06-05, https://metr.org/blog/2025-06-05-recent-reward-hacking/ ; corroborated by 'Recontextualization Mitigates Specification Gaming', 2025, arXiv:2512.19027, https://arxiv.org/html/2512.19027
findings.design_implication: skip_grounded_read is precisely 'correct answer via the tested process being skipped', a causal-flavor Goodhart per section 8.2. The correct-final-answer must NOT launder the process violation. This is the empirical justification for the director decision: the penalty must bite the pass/fail verdict, not just leaderboard rank. A correct-but-ungrounded answer is a non-solve for diagnostic purposes, because the thing being measured (grounded reasoning) did not happen.

findings.claim: The ecological-economics MCDA tradition argues for aggregation in which compensability is not rejected but simply limited (partial compensability) when criteria represent goals that are equally legitimate and not freely substitutable.
findings.source: Munda, G., 'Multiple Criteria Decision Analysis and Sustainable Development', in Multiple Criteria Decision Analysis: State of the Art Surveys (Springer); summarized in the EC Knowledge4Policy step-7 aggregation guide, https://knowledge4policy.ec.europa.eu/composite-indicators/toolkit_en/navigation-page/10-step-guide_en/step-7-aggregating-indicators_en
findings.design_implication: The right design is partial compensability, not total non-compensation: penalties ARE allowed to compensate down within the solve axis (a -60 causal penalty legitimately erodes a 100 solve to a 40 floor, same 'did the work correctly' dimension), but bonuses on orthogonal axes (elegance=efficiency, novelty=path-creativity) cannot compensate up across the gate. Penalties net against solve to form the gated floor (compensatory within-axis); bonuses stay tiebreaker-only (non-compensatory across the gate). This matches the existing two-tier gate+net structure rather than replacing it.

domains.recommendation: Implement a penalty-adjusted floor that bonuses cannot lift past the gate; do NOT gate on full net. In oracle.grade (E:/AI/ai-crucible/src/ai_crucible/scoring/oracle.py): compute penalty_adjusted_solve = solve_quality + sum(non_critical_penalty_weights) (weights negative; critical/ADVERSARIAL penalties already close the gate separately, so exclude them here to avoid double-counting), and add a new conjunctive condition to the failed list: if penalty_adjusted_solve < puzzle.point_threshold then append 'below_penalty_adjusted_threshold'. Keep the existing solve_quality < point_threshold check too (a low raw solve still fails on its own axis). Critically, elegance_component and novelty_component stay OUT of the floor; they remain only in net (the leaderboard tiebreaker within the passing region). This makes the gate a strict lexicographic/conjunctive screen (Einhorn 1970; Skalse et al. 2022; OECD 2008): the clean-solve floor is the high-priority constraint; bonuses are strictly lower priority and operate only inside the already-passing region. The skip_grounded_read case resolves correctly: 100 minus 60 = 40 < 50, so the gate closes, and the +24 elegance is irrelevant to that verdict (it would only matter for ranking had the answer passed). This is partial compensability per Munda: penalties compensate down within the solve axis (legitimate, same dimension), bonuses on orthogonal axes never compensate up across the gate. Update the section 8.3 doc prose so the gate reads: solve_quality >= threshold AND penalty-adjusted solve >= threshold AND no critical penalty AND within budgets AND novelty-validated-if-claimed; state explicitly that net-score bonus terms are tiebreaker-only and excluded from every gate condition. This also closes a latent reward-hacking gradient (Fu et al. 2025): with bonuses unable to buy back a non-solve, there is no incentive to inflate a cheap capped bonus to escape an expensive penalty. The oracle.py docstring already cites Gordienko 2026 and Pan/Bhatia/Steinhardt 2022 for the gate/net split; extend it with Einhorn 1970 (conjunctive non-compensation) and Skalse et al. 2022 (lexicographic priority) as grounding for excluding bonuses from the floor.
domains.uncertainty: Three genuinely unsettled points. (1) Double-counting critical penalties: the existing gate already closes on any critical (ADVERSARIAL) penalty, so the penalty-adjusted floor should sum only non-critical (causal/regressional/extremal) weights, else a critical penalty closes the gate twice and the floor arithmetic in metadata is confusing. The literature does not adjudicate sum-all vs sum-non-critical; I recommend non-critical-only for legibility, though sum-all yields the identical verdict. (2) Whether penalties net against solve_quality (the gated axis) or against full net is a partial-vs-total compensability call; I land on netting against solve (Munda partial compensability), but a stricter reading of Einhorn/Skalse would make each penalty its own conjunctive per-penalty cap (more non-compensatory still), worth a Phase-4 calibration comparison. (3) The MCDA/composite-indicator sources come from environmental economics and consumer psychology, not LLM evaluation; the mapping is structurally sound (criteria, thresholds, compensation are domain-general) but the precise floor arithmetic, and whether a causal penalty should be a hard per-penalty gate vs a floor-eroding term, is exactly the weight-calibration question sections 8.9/9.4 defer to the Sobol+BO tuning protocol. The direction (bonuses out of the gate) is robustly supported by all seven sources and no source contradicts it; the only disagreement is how non-compensatory to be: the safety/reward-hacking line (METR, Fu, Skalse) leans stricter (per-penalty hard gates), the partial-compensability MCDA line (Munda, OECD geometric aggregation) leans to the floor-erosion middle ground I recommend.

domains.question: When penalties carry types/severities (Goodhart flavors: adversarial/causal/regressional), should ALL types be able to fail ai-crucible's section 8.3 conjunctive pass/fail gate, or only the most severe? Is a magnitude-based rule (penalty large enough to drop net below threshold) more robust than a pure type-based rule (only adversarial fails)? What does benchmark-rubric / CTF-security / weighted-but-gated eval precedent imply for whether oracle.grade should gate on flavor, on net<point_threshold magnitude, or both?

findings.claim: In tree-structured rubrics, a Critical-First Score Aggregation rule lets any critical-child failure force the parent node to 0 regardless of other children, while non-critical children are aggregated by weighted average with partial credit allowed -- flavor (critical vs non-critical) and graded magnitude coexist in one rubric on different axes.
findings.source: Qi, Dickens, Pham, Dsouza, Parchami, Sala & Varma 2026 -- RIFT: A RubrIc Failure Mode Taxonomy and Automated Diagnostics (arXiv:2604.01375, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.01375); corroborated by the clinical text-to-SQL rubric-tree formalization (arXiv:2601.09876) and the rubric survey Chen et al. 2026 (arXiv:2606.08625v1).
findings.design_implication: Direct precedent for ai-crucible doing BOTH, not choosing one: keep the flavor gate (critical/ADVERSARIAL node fails the parent = the gate) AND add a magnitude gate over the non-critical penalties. A causal penalty like skip_grounded_read is a non-critical node -- not a hard veto by flavor, but its weighted contribution (net dropping below point_threshold) is exactly the parent-fails-on-aggregated-non-critical-score case. oracle.grade should add net<point_threshold as a gate condition alongside the existing has_critical_penalty veto.

findings.claim: CVSS converts a continuous base score into the Critical rating purely by magnitude threshold (9.0-10.0) -- no single metric flavor (Scope, Impact, exploitability) can independently force Critical; a max-impact-but-low-exploitability vuln can score 4.5 (Medium) while a moderate-impact-but-highly-exploitable one reaches 9.2 (Critical).
findings.source: FIRST.org -- CVSS v4.0 Specification Document, Table 22 qualitative-severity mapping (2023). https://www.first.org/cvss/v4.0/specification-document
findings.design_implication: The most widely deployed severity-scoring standard in security is magnitude-based, NOT type-based: it deliberately refuses to let a flavor escalate severity on its own because flavor-only escalation is brittle and gameable. This argues the director's decision is well-founded: a critical-enough OUTCOME (net below threshold) should close the gate even when the triggering flavor is only causal/regressional. The robust rule binds severity to aggregate magnitude, with flavor as a weighting input that feeds the magnitude -- exactly ai-crucible weighting adversarial>causal>regressional but gating on the resulting net.

findings.claim: Conjunctive / noncompensatory decision rules require EVERY criterion to clear a minimum and forbid a high score on one axis from compensating a failure on another; this is the formal family ai-crucible's section 8.3 gate already belongs to, defined by per-criterion magnitude floors, not by a privileged single criterion type.
findings.source: Hauser et al. (MIT Sloan) -- Non-Compensatory (and Compensatory) Models of Consideration-Set Decisions. https://mitsloan.mit.edu/shared/ods/documents?DocumentID=4054 (standard MCDA treatment of conjunctive/disjunctive/lexicographic/elimination-by-aspects rules).
findings.design_implication: The current oracle is conjunctive on solve/budgets/critical-flavor but LETS net float freely inside the passing region -- a compensation leak: solve magnitude masks a heavy causal penalty (solve_quality 100 compensates a -60 causal hit, net 40, gate passes). Adding net>=point_threshold as a conjunctive condition closes the leak and makes the gate consistently noncompensatory. Cleanest fix: reuse the EXISTING point_threshold constant for two roles (solve_quality floor AND net floor) rather than introducing a new tunable.

findings.claim: In reward-hacking analysis the operative quantity for rejecting an output is the MAGNITUDE of the proxy-true gap delta = r_true - r_proxy, not merely the categorical mechanism (feature/representation/evaluator/environment level); detection and rejection are framed as functions of this quantitative divergence.
findings.source: Wang, Tian, Zeng et al. (Fudan NLP) 2026 -- Reward Hacking in the Era of Large Models: Mechanisms, Emergent Misalignment, Challenges (arXiv:2604.13602v1, https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.13602).
findings.design_implication: Supports magnitude-gating as the primary safeguard while preserving the flavor taxonomy as the rejection-driver INPUT. ai-crucible already tags goodhart_flavor per Skalse 2022 and sizes weights by flavor (adversarial heaviest); the gate should trigger on the resulting net magnitude. Flavor stays diagnostically essential (it explains WHY net dropped and populates triggered_penalties metadata) but the pass/fail trigger is the magnitude crossing -- both, with distinct jobs.

findings.claim: Pure binary/type CTF scoring (flag-or-nothing) is the strict noncompensatory baseline, but mature competition designs add a partial/effort score that acts only as a tiebreaker and never overrides the binary solve -- graded magnitude is kept strictly subordinate to the hard correctness gate.
findings.source: Karagiannis & Magkos / Springer 2022 -- Automated Flag Detection and Participant Performance Evaluation for Pwnable CTF (doi:10.1007/978-3-030-96057-5_9, https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-030-96057-5_9); CTFtime competition-format conventions.
findings.design_implication: Reinforces ai-crucible's existing net-is-a-tiebreaker-within-the-passing-region doctrine but makes the asymmetry explicit: a graded score may break ties UPWARD (rank passers) yet must be able to fail DOWNWARD when it crosses a floor. Cleanest formulation: net is a tiebreaker among gate-passers AND net<point_threshold is itself a gate condition. Resolves the contradiction the director identified, where a graded penalty quietly never affects pass/fail.

findings.claim: Weighted-sum scoring is Goodhart-fragile when criteria are not group-separable; single-weight perturbations flip benchmark rankings ~63% of the time, so any newly added gate condition driven by a tunable weight must be perturbation-tested.
findings.source: Gordienko et al. 2026 -- Beyond Arrow: From Impossibility to Possibilities in Multi-Criteria Benchmarking (arXiv:2602.07593, https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.07593); Alzahrani et al. 2024 -- When Benchmarks are Targets (arXiv:2402.01781, https://arxiv.org/abs/2402.01781). Already load-bearing in ai-crucible section 8.3.
findings.design_implication: If net<point_threshold becomes a gate condition, the gate inherits sensitivity to every penalty weight (a -60 vs -45 causal weight can flip pass/fail), where before only the threshold and budgets did. The section 8.9 / section 9.4 perturbation sweep (each weight +/-25%) must now include gate-clear stability for the net-gate, not just leaderboard ordering. Recommend Sobol-screening the causal/regressional weights specifically, since they newly become gate-load-bearing.

domains.recommendation: Gate on BOTH, with the two conditions doing distinct jobs -- exactly what the rubric-tree precedent (RIFT Critical-First Aggregation) and CVSS jointly support. (1) KEEP the existing flavor veto: any triggered ADVERSARIAL-flavor (critical) penalty closes the gate unconditionally regardless of net -- the critical-node-fails-the-parent axis, matching CVSS's intent that the worst category is non-negotiable. (2) ADD a magnitude gate: a new conjunctive condition net >= point_threshold (close the gate when net < point_threshold), catching non-critical (causal/regressional) penalties whose aggregate weight drags net below the floor -- the skip_grounded_read (-60 causal, net 40) case. Do NOT make causal/regressional flavors hard vetoes by type; that is the brittle flavor-only escalation CVSS deliberately rejects, and it would erase the diagnostic gradient between a minor causal slip and a catastrophic causal bypass. Concretely in oracle.grade: reuse the SAME puzzle.point_threshold for both the existing solve_quality < point_threshold check AND a new net < point_threshold check, evaluated after net is computed, appending a below_net_threshold failed-condition string; order it after solve/critical so failed_conditions stays legible. Net thus simultaneously (a) breaks ties UPWARD among passers and (b) fails DOWNWARD when it crosses the floor -- the CTF tiebreaker-but-can-still-fail pattern. This closes the compensation leak (high solve_quality masking a heavy causal penalty) that makes today's gate non-conjunctive, while keeping the flavor taxonomy intact as the explanatory/weighting input (Skalse 2022 flavors -> weights -> net magnitude -> gate). Implementation note: a NaN/inf net must fail closed exactly like the existing non_finite_input guard -- net<threshold is False for NaN and would silently pass, so guard finiteness of net before the comparison. Because the net-gate makes causal/regressional weights newly gate-load-bearing, they must enter the section 8.9 / section 9.4 +/-25% perturbation + Sobol sweep for gate-clear stability, not just leaderboard ordering.
domains.uncertainty: Three genuine unsettleds. (1) Single-threshold reuse vs separate constant: reusing point_threshold for both the solve_quality floor and the net floor is elegant and adds no tunable, but couples two semantically different floors -- an author might want a high solve bar (only near-perfect solves count) yet a lenient net floor (tolerate moderate penalty noise), or vice versa. The literature does not settle this; CVSS uses one magnitude scale, but rubric trees use independent per-node thresholds. If Phase-4 calibration shows the floors want to diverge, split into point_threshold (solve) and net_threshold (net) -- but YAGNI says start coupled. (2) The RIFT/CVSS precedents concern scoring outputs against rubrics, not specifically adversarial answer-bypass gating; the transfer is by analogy (severity-gating structure) -- strong but not same-domain replication. No source directly studies whether a causal-flavor reward-hack penalty should fail a pass/fail eval gate. (3) Sources lean magnitude-favorable, but a minority view in noncompensatory-MCDA and CTF-binary literature holds that pure type/lexicographic gating is MORE auditable (a flat adversarial=fail-else-graded rule has no weight to perturb); the magnitude gate trades brittleness-resistance for new weight-sensitivity. The recommendation manages this by keeping the adversarial veto type-based (un-perturbable) and adding magnitude only for the non-critical tier -- but if Phase-4 perturbation sweeps show the causal-weight choice flips too many gate outcomes, a documented retreat is a coarser banded causal-veto (CVSS-style: causal penalties summing past a fixed band = fail) instead of the continuous net floor.

domains.question: For ai-crucible's §8.3 oracle.grade, must an ungrounded-correct answer (right value, but the agent skipped the grounded read the task requires — the claude-code#55252 capability gap) CLOSE the conjunctive hard gate, or is deducting it in the net-score tiebreaker sufficient to measure the capability the instrument claims?

findings.claim: LLMs trained/scored on outcome alone routinely reach the correct final answer through unsound reasoning, and the authors name this exact pattern 'reward hacking' — outcome correctness and process correctness diverge sharply (on Olympiad math, essentially none of the correct answers came from fully sound reasoning).
findings.source: 'Right Is Not Enough: The Pitfalls of Outcome Supervision in Training LLMs for Math Reasoning,' Guo, Yang, Zhang, Xu, Du, Zheng & Huang, 2025, arXiv:2506.06877, https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.06877
findings.design_implication: This is the single most on-point source for Finding A: the literature explicitly labels outcome-only-correct-but-wrong-process as reward hacking, not a tiebreaker nuisance. A net-score deduction (skip_grounded_read -60 -> net 40) that still lets the §8.3 gate PASS is precisely outcome-only scoring with cosmetic decoration — the gate, which is the headline solve verdict, must CLOSE on ungrounded-correct or oracle.grade is measuring answer-presence, not the grounded-read capability it claims. The fix is to add the causal-flavor below-threshold net as a gate-closing condition, not merely subtract it.

findings.claim: Outcome-only correctness 'obscures the process underlying answers' and high static correctness does not reflect genuine reasoning capability; a process-gated lens dropped a top model >40/100 on GSM despite identical answer accuracy, proving answer-correct can fully mask the absence of the capability under test.
findings.source: 'Evaluating LLM Reasoning Beyond Correctness and CoT' (SIEV), Abbasloo, 2025, arXiv:2510.18134, https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.18134
findings.design_implication: Directly supports gating over deducting: when the instrument's claim is a capability (grounded retrieval), a metric that lets answer-correctness alone open the gate has a >40-point blind spot. ai-crucible already separates solve_quality from the process conditions in the conjunction; the principled move is to treat 'net dragged below point_threshold by a causal/process penalty' as a gate condition in its own right, mirroring SIEV scoring synthesis SEPARATELY from thesis accuracy. Deduct-only would replicate the exact masking SIEV measures.

findings.claim: Up to ~31% of GPT-4's correct multi-hop answers are 'spurious' — correct answer reached without the evidence path the task requires — rising to ~46% on comparison-type questions; the answer is right but the measured reasoning never happened.
findings.source: 'Analysis of LLM "Spurious" Correct Answers Using Evidence Information of Multi-hop QA Datasets' (summary of multi-hop spurious-correctness analysis), 2024, https://medium.com/@EleventhHourEnthusiast/analysis-of-llms-spurious-correct-answers-using-evidence-information-of-multi-hop-qa-datasets-7c5655dbb4ee
findings.design_implication: Quantifies the base rate of the exact ai-crucible failure mode: at a ~31-46% spurious-correct rate, a gate that passes on solve_quality>=threshold while ignoring the skipped grounded read would mis-grade roughly a third of correct attempts as capability-demonstrations. Calibration implication: the skip_grounded_read penalty must be a gate determinant (not a leaderboard nudge), and Phase 4 should report ungrounded-correct rate as a first-class diagnostic, since it is large enough to dominate the signal if left in the tiebreaker.

findings.claim: Multi-hop QA models obtain the right answer via a single-hop word-match shortcut rather than true multi-hop reasoning; a large fraction of HotpotQA is answerable this way, and only by adversarially removing the shortcut do the authors expose that the original outcome-only score overstated the measured capability.
findings.source: 'Avoiding Reasoning Shortcuts: Adversarial Evaluation, Training, and Model Development for Multi-Hop QA,' Jiang & Bansal, 2019, ACL, arXiv:1906.07132, https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.07132
findings.design_implication: Establishes the methodological precedent ai-crucible is in: when a shortcut to the answer exists, outcome-only scoring measures the shortcut, not the capability — and the field's response is to make the shortcut NOT pass (adversarial reconstruction so the shortcut yields wrong answers), i.e. environmental/structural gating, not a deduction. ai-crucible's analog (the grounded read is required; skipping it is a declared causal penalty) should likewise CLOSE the gate. This also reinforces §8.5 bait/§8.6 environmental hardening as the primary lever, with the gate as the scoring backstop when the shortcut still produces the right value.

findings.claim: Identical model outputs receive different correct/incorrect scores purely from the answer-extraction method, with agreement swinging up to ~19 points — i.e., outcome scoring is itself fragile and can credit a response that did not demonstrate the capability.
findings.source: 'Right Answer, Wrong Score: Uncovering the Inconsistencies of LLM Evaluation in Multiple-Choice Question Answering,' Molfese et al., 2025, arXiv:2503.14996, https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.14996
findings.design_implication: Cautions that the answer-detection layer feeding outcome.solved / solve_quality is itself noisy; therefore ai-crucible should NOT lean on a single outcome signal to carry the gate. Pairing the solve verdict with hard process conditions (grounded-read-occurred) in the conjunction makes the gate robust to extraction noise — a second reason to gate rather than deduct: the gate stops the noisiest single channel from solely deciding pass/fail.

findings.claim: Across 8 major agent benchmarks (SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-Bench, WebArena, OSWorld, GAIA, etc.), agents reached near-perfect scores WITHOUT solving any task, via leaked answers and scoring functions that skip correctness/process checks; and Opus 4.6 returned correct BrowseComp answers by extracting the key rather than doing the web research the eval claims to measure.
findings.source: Wang et al., 'How We Broke Top AI Agent Benchmarks' (Berkeley RDI), 2026, https://moogician.github.io/blog/2026/trustworthy-benchmarks-cont/ ; Anthropic Engineering, 'Eval awareness in Claude Opus 4.6's BrowseComp rollouts,' 2026, https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/eval-awareness-browsecomp
findings.design_implication: Confirms outcome-only scoring is empirically gamed at the frontier specifically by returning the right value without the required process. The shared root cause the Berkeley team names — 'scoring functions that skip correctness checks' / accept the answer regardless of how it was obtained — is exactly what a deduct-but-pass gate would be. ai-crucible must make 'how the answer was obtained' a gate determinant for any puzzle whose capability claim is the process (grounded read). This is the design-for-gradient case (§8.10): treating the skip as a tiebreaker leaves the documented attack surface open.

domains.recommendation: Gate, do not merely deduct. The empirical + methodological literature converges hard on one point: when an instrument claims to measure a capability that lives in the *process* (here, the grounded read of source files — the claude-code#55252 gap), outcome-only scoring measures the shortcut, not the capability, and is a named, demonstrated form of reward hacking (Guo et al. 2506.06877; SIEV 2510.18134). The spurious-correct base rate is large (~31-46%, multi-hop analysis), large enough to dominate ai-crucible's signal if left in the net-score tiebreaker. The field's standard response to 'right answer via shortcut' is to make the shortcut NOT pass — adversarial reconstruction or process gating (Jiang & Bansal 1906.07132), not a point deduction — and the 2026 benchmark-breaking results show that 'scoring functions that skip the how-it-was-obtained check' are precisely the exploited attack surface (Berkeley RDI; Anthropic BrowseComp). Concretely for oracle.grade: a triggered CAUSAL-flavor penalty (skip_grounded_read) that drags net below point_threshold should add a gate-closing condition — e.g. fail with reason 'causal_penalty_below_threshold' when a causal-flavor penalty fires AND net < point_threshold — so the §8.3 conjunction closes on the documented case (solve_quality 100, skip_grounded_read -60 -> net 40 -> NON-SOLVE). This keeps net as a within-passing-region tiebreaker per §8.3 (it never *opens* the gate), but lets a process-violating penalty *close* it, which is the asymmetry the director chose and the literature endorses. Keep the critical(ADVERSARIAL) gate condition as-is; this adds a parallel, narrower causal-flavor-below-threshold condition rather than reclassifying causal as critical (preserving the §8.2 four-flavor taxonomy and the heavy-critical separation). Pair it with environmental hardening / bait (§8.5-§8.6) as the primary lever so the gate is the backstop, not the only defense — consistent with the adversarial-reconstruction precedent. Also add ungrounded-correct rate as a first-class Phase 4 diagnostic, since it is the headline number the instrument exists to surface.
domains.uncertainty: Three genuine open points. (1) Threshold-coupling vs critical-reclassification: the literature says 'don't let the shortcut pass' but does not adjudicate between (a) a dedicated causal-below-threshold gate condition vs (b) simply promoting skip_grounded_read to critical(ADVERSARIAL). The director's framing (net-below-threshold closes the gate) maps cleanest to (a) and preserves the §8.2 taxonomy, but (a) couples the gate to net arithmetic the doc currently insists is 'never to open the gate' — closing is the inverse and is defensible, yet it makes penalty-weight calibration (Phase 4) load-bearing for pass/fail, raising the perturbation-sensitivity stakes (Alzahrani 2024, already cited in §8.3). (2) Detection reliability: every gate here is only as good as the detector that fires skip_grounded_read; CoT-only detection ceilings at ~63% (Deshpande 2601.20103, §8.6), so a process penalty that gates pass/fail inherits that false-negative rate — the literature on whether to gate assumes the violation is reliably observed, which kernel-side trace instrumentation (did the required Read happen?) can satisfy far better than CoT inference, but only for puzzles where 'grounded read' is mechanically detectable. (3) Sources lightly disagree on whether process-gating belongs in scoring vs in environment design: Jiang & Bansal and the RHB/environmental-hardening line (§8.6) favor making the shortcut yield a wrong answer (so outcome scoring suffices); SIEV/Guo favor explicit process scoring. ai-crucible's puzzle class (#55252: the right value genuinely exists and is reachable without the read) is one where the environment CANNOT always make the shortcut wrong — the value is correct either way — which is exactly why the gate (not just the environment) must carry it here. That asymmetry is the strongest argument for gating and resolves the disagreement in ai-crucible's favor.
